title: Bomber County Gateway Lancaster Memorial
aircraft_type: Full-size Lancaster replica (steel frame) commemorating Lancaster
memorial_type: Full-size Lancaster representation at road junction

maintainer.notes: Full-size Lancaster representation maintained by the Bomber Gateway Trust.

external_narratives.summary: The Bomber Gateway Trust created 'On Freedom's Wings', a full-scale steel-frame representation of an Avro Lancaster Mk1 bomber measuring 26 metres in length with a 31-metre wingspan. The sculpture commemorates Lancaster R5689 VN-N, described as the most photographed Lancaster of the war, which crashed on 18 September 1942 returning from a Baltic Sea mission with the loss of five crew.
key_details: Taller than Angel of the North
R5689 described as most photographed Lancaster of the war

external_narratives.summary: The RAF's official website describes the On Freedom's Wings sculpture as a landmark honouring the service and sacrifice of Bomber Command personnel, located near Norton Disney close to the A46.
key_details: Endorsed by RAF Battle of Britain Memorial Flight
Located near Norton Disney, close to RAF Swinderby
